front: Describe the Hill equation and its use in pharmacology.
back: Hill equation: E = Emax x [C]^n / (EC50^n + [C]^n). n (Hill coefficient) indicates cooperativity: n=1 (hyperbolic, no cooperativity), n>1 (positive cooperativity, sigmoidal), n<1 (negative cooperativity). Used for dose-response curve fitting.
